title: UN Security Council votes 12-2 to endorse Black Sea grain export deal extension
summary: The UN Security Council voted 12-2 (Russia and China abstaining) to endorse a fragile Black Sea grain export deal extension amid renewed Russian strikes. US Ambassador Thomas-Greenfield called the outcome 'a step, not a solution.'
contents:
# UNSC Endorses Black Sea Grain Deal Extension in 12-2 Vote

## Vote Outcome
The United Nations Security Council voted 12-2 to endorse an extension of the Black Sea grain export deal. Russia and China abstained from the vote. The resolution passed on April 30, 2026.

## Key Statements
US Ambassador Linda Thomas-Greenfield characterized the result as "a step, not a solution," emphasizing the fragile nature of the agreement amid renewed Russian military strikes in the region.

## Upcoming Session
The Security Council scheduled its next session for May 3 to address Gaza aid blockades. Additional routine briefings covered the Sudan famine crisis (20 million people at risk) and Haiti gang violence.

## Haiti Peacekeeping
France pushed for deploying a peacekeeping force to Haiti to address gang violence. Russia threatened to veto the proposal, creating another diplomatic flashpoint.

## Broader Context
The grain deal extension comes at a critical juncture as Ukrainian agricultural exports face continued disruption from the ongoing conflict. The deal serves as one of few remaining cooperative frameworks between the warring parties.
